sections: HCM-1.2 — Instance profiles and artifact descriptors
Task 18: Full proof, review, promotion, and closeout

repo_truth: Review the exact 92-entry eleven-times-remediated manifest over baseline 83ccaabcaa2481b33bc58a69736a285b9c714e00. The manifest is rebuilt as the union of every live baseline-to-subject modified or added path and the retained authority/support entries, excluding only this transport envelope; it now includes the artifact-kind registry test omitted from Review 11. Reviews 1 through 10 returned thirty-two Required findings in total. Review 11 returned BLOCKED for manifest integrity plus two Required findings; the parent accepted and remediated all thirty-four findings, replayed the complete proof wall after every round, and preserved all prior dispatches as immutable lineage. HCM-1.1 remains the completed dependency boundary and the selected planning handoff remains transition/packet-approval evidence only. The subject implements only HCM-1.2 additively in handbook-engine. Current product paths remain unchanged and HCM-1.3 is not active. Derive the verdict only from live authority, exact manifest replay, all prior review surfaces, and independent read-only verification.
allowed_scope: Remain read-only and replay every sorted subject-manifest entry plus aggregate before review.
Review every HCM-1.2 engine implementation, package asset, test/fixture, packet/status/proof byte, package manifest, bounded crosswalk row, and bounded gate-evidence byte in the manifest.
Read unchanged canonical authority, selected handoff/ledger, HCM-1.1 dependency implementation, Cargo metadata, platform APIs, and official dependency documentation only as needed.
Run read-only focused, workspace, cross-target compile, package, dependency-feature, archive, handoff, Git, source, security, and semantic checks needed to evaluate the subject.
Return findings first through the built-in channel with exact file and line evidence.
non_goals: No edits, staging, commits, new dispatches, global handoffs, ledger writes, or user-task hops.
No current layout/canonical-artifact/setup/doctor/flow/compiler/CLI/SDK/Tauri/Substrate/materialization/product adoption and no HCM-1.3 work.
No Context Resolution policy execution, condition evaluation, vocabulary application, intake, lifecycle, renderer, Projection, publication, or downstream behavior.
Do not infer unspecified schemas, identifiers, fingerprints, defaults, compatibility, ambient discovery, ranges/latest, legacy behavior, or later-slice semantics.
Do not close PG-PROFILE-01, PG-ARTIFACT-01, PG-KIND-01, or PG-KIND-02.
Do not require stylistic alternatives without a violated authority, invariant, security boundary, proof gate, or concrete defect.
tasks: Invoke the required skills, remain read-only, replay the exact sorted manifest and aggregate, and return BLOCKED if any subject byte differs.

Independently re-audit every Review-1 Required surface: immutable package bytes, retained one-read admission, combined definition/schema budget, declared-ref pairing, deferred topological profile fingerprints, query-wide binding-shape traversal, typed public errors, complete public descriptor metadata, repository scope, URI/drive path refusal, and corresponding proof.

Independently re-audit every Review-2 Required surface: the exact 32-edge ancestry boundary; descriptor error kind/location distinctions; duplicate schema/kind refs within authored replacements; condition-source derivation from selected-ancestry descriptors; vocabulary stable-role producer resolution/equality/propagation; and the focused positive and negative proof for each.

Independently re-audit every Review-3 Required surface: root-to-leaf complete-field replacement with distinct admitted-union and final effective schema/kind registries; exact capability-contract producer and capability-ID validation before provider counting; conservative binding-shape refusal for pattern-opened parents and object terminals; and the focused positive and negative proof for each.

Independently re-audit every Review-4 Required surface: immutable built-in source reservation for every package-owned exact ref and the complete shipped-profile drift matrix; contradiction refusal for supported object/array/string binding terminals; and compound proof of all ten fail-fast stages, every frozen Stage-8 producer boundary, closure-before-stale-fingerprint behavior, exact error kind/location, and reversed source order where relevant.

Independently re-audit every Review-5 Required surface: explicit presence of all nullable closed descriptor members before typed decoding; satisfiability of every root, referenced, and intermediate binding parent; and Stage-5 closed syntactic validation of all eleven authored profile fields before Stage-6 cycle handling.

Independently re-audit every Review-6 Required surface: conservative satisfiability of every traversed schema location and transitively required child including exact refs, dependentRequired, propertyNames, and Draft 2020-12 default minContains semantics; and exact typed/location separation of descriptor schema/version failures from later-owned dependency selections.

Independently re-audit every Review-7 Required surface: centralized joint schema satisfiability including effective minContains versus maxItems and provably nameable closed-object minProperties at terminals and transitively required references; and Stage-5 closed decoding of nested artifact-kind semantic capability records before ancestry, including unknown-field taxonomy and reversed source order.

Independently re-audit every Review-8 Required surface: joint satisfiability witnesses for numeric bounds, items with effective contains, closed-object minProperties with only satisfiable name/schema pairs, bounded string patterns, and transitively required referenced siblings; refuse whenever the combined supported constraints are not proven satisfiable.

Independently re-audit the Review-9 Required surface: conservative satisfiability for uniqueItems against finite item domains, required prefixItems, maxContains when required slots necessarily match, and triggered dependentSchemas at direct terminals and transitively required referenced siblings; verify the exact 149-test recount and all focused positive and negative controls.

Independently re-audit both Review-10 Required surfaces: prove a single joint Draft 2020-12 array witness across uniqueItems, contains, prefixItems, items, and cardinality constraints within explicit fail-closed proof budgets; and count optional minProperties witnesses only after their complete dependentRequired closure and triggered dependentSchemas remain compatible with property schemas and maxProperties. Verify direct terminals, transitively required references, all four exact negatives, retained positive boundaries, and the exact 151-test recount.

Independently re-audit both Review-11 Required surfaces and its subject-integrity stop: prove unevaluatedItems participates in required-item joint array satisfiability at direct and transitively referenced locations; validate all five later-owned fields on every selected ancestry source without disturbing the frozen Stage-9-before-Stage-10 precedence; verify shadowed parent negatives, explicit-empty and inherited positives in both orders, the exact 155-test recount, and confirm the manifest includes every live baseline-to-subject modified or added path including crates/engine/tests/artifact_kind_registry.rs.

Review findings first in Critical, Required, Optional, Nit order. Each actionable finding must include severity, exact file/line, violated contract or invariant, concrete consequence, smallest valid remediation, and missing verification.

Audit the exact request/source envelope, SymbolicId and canonical SemVer grammars, typed source-class/ref matching, fail-fast order, source accounting, and every N/N+1 count, path, document, depth, and byte ceiling.

Audit safe repository source admission and descriptor paths for exact normalization, containment, no-follow retained-handle behavior, bounded reads/errors, platform branches, and absolute/traversal/symlink/non-regular/URI/oversize refusal.

Literally compare all six schema records to SPEC: exact closed fields, bounds, set semantics, stable refs, duplicate behavior, Environment secret impossibility, and absence of canonical content normalization/fingerprinting.
Audit the request-wide schema closure budget and binding_shape query for shared-identity counting, 128/129, depth, root/target path limits, determinate closed traversal, and no raw schema escape.

Literally compare all six kinds, the exact nine constitutional binding rules, validator/capability/condition/vocabulary/policy/stack producers, dependency direction, compatibility, record closure, fingerprints, and non-execution boundaries.

Audit all three artifact descriptors, requiredness/condition truth, role/capability compatibility, normalized unique paths, dependency namespace/contract/cardinality/providers/cycles, later-owned emptiness, and exactly one always-required constitutional root.

Audit authored profile parsing and all eleven replace-whole fields across root/named/repository ancestry, scope ordering, cycle/depth/source limits, deterministic decisions, explicit empty clearing, and rejection of partial merge/tombstone/fallback semantics.

Audit exact leaf selection and resolved fingerprint closure: every selected-ancestor dependency must be admitted and used even when shadowed; genuinely unrelated, missing, duplicate/conflicting, wrong-class/ref, unallowlisted built-in, range/latest, ambient, legacy, and invocation inputs must refuse deterministically.

Literally prove the shipped root is the exact HCM-0.6 six-kind/three-instance set with no Work/Decision/Risk root instance, and verify repository whole-field replacement without product-path adoption.

Verify all semantic bytes and referenced fingerprints affect the appropriate definition/registry/profile closure; stress stale/forged fingerprints, changed bytes behind a ref, order permutation, and closure cycles/back-edges.

Replay or inspect every positive, N/N+1, negative, security, engine/workspace, cross-target, dependency, package, archive, handoff, diff/scope, and GitNexus assertion in the proof; identify any asserted case not actually exercised or any required proof absent from the manifest.

Verify literal tree/.crate equality for all 29 definition assets using exact member names, sizes, hashes, and bytes; verify no Cargo manifest/lock change and no network/TLS/async/executable-hook capability.
Verify the crosswalk and proof ledger promote only the exact additive metadata/resolution boundaries to BoundaryLanded while all named gates and every product-adoption behavior remain open.
Return CLEAN only if no valid Critical or Required finding remains; otherwise return CHANGES_REQUIRED or BLOCKED. Make no edits.
stop_conditions: Return BLOCKED if the manifest, aggregate, entry authority, or required read-only verification cannot be reproduced.
Return CHANGES_REQUIRED rather than resolving a missing schema, fingerprint, default, compatibility, product adoption, policy execution, or later-slice decision by inference.
Return to the parent if remediation would require HCM-1.3+, a current product path, a sibling crate, or authority outside the exact HCM-1.2 packet.
